beats.activity: Wakes up, showers, throws on clothes, grabs coffee and toast at home.
beats.reasoning: Jesse sleeps in late as a night owl; 9:15am is realistic for him. Quick 30-min morning routine with minimal fussing.

beats.activity: Skateboards to work through Capitol Hill streets.
beats.reasoning: Jesse skateboards to his barista job; Capitol Hill to Capitol Hill is a short 20-min ride, fits his impulsive transportation style.

beats.activity: Starts shift at Victrola, prepping espresso machines and taking orders.
beats.reasoning: Friday morning shift at the coffee shop; he's on the clock before the 11am FIFA match airs.

beats.activity: Watches USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match on the shop's TV during break, chatting with coworkers and regulars.
beats.reasoning: Jesse is impulsive and loves sports; the 11am match is live and he catches it at work where there's guaranteed TV access and crowd energy. He's not the type to skip it.

beats.activity: Continues barista shift, pulling shots and chatting with customers.
beats.reasoning: Finishes his Friday shift; 3.5-hour total shift is typical for part-time work ending mid-afternoon.

beats.activity: Skateboards to Whole Foods to grab snacks and energy drinks.
beats.reasoning: Jesse stays local in Capitol Hill; quick errand for junk food and drinks he'll need for the evening. Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect him.

beats.activity: Walks back home, takes a breather, scrolls phone, listens to music.; Sketches and practices graffiti-style art at home, blasting hip-hop and rap.
beats.reasoning: Jesse needs downtime between work and evening plans; he gets anxious in quiet spaces so he'll have music playing. Short walk home with groceries.

beats.activity: Showers, changes into fresh clothes, grabs wallet and keys.
beats.reasoning: Jesse preps for a Friday night out; he's impulsive and restless, eager to hit the town with friends or alone.

beats.activity: Skateboards to an arcade bar for drinks and games with friends.
beats.reasoning: Jesse loves arcade bars and loud music; Ground Kontrol in Downtown is a real spot that fits his vibe. 25-min skateboard ride from Capitol Hill.

beats.activity: Plays arcade games, drinks beer, hangs with friends, soaks up the chaotic energy.
beats.reasoning: Jesse thrives in high-energy social settings. A long Friday night at an arcade bar is perfect for his restless, loyal personality. Ends before midnight.

beats.activity: Skateboards home through the warm June night.
beats.reasoning: Jesse heads home after a night out; 35-min late-night skateboard ride from Downtown to Capitol Hill. Sunny early June weather makes this plausible.

beats.activity: Winds down with late-night snacks and music, then crashes hard into sleep.
beats.reasoning: Jesse collapses after a full day of work and partying. Sleeps 7.5 hours, ending well before 6am the next morning. Spans overnight as required.

diary:
Monday was one of those days that just flows right, you know? Woke up early, grabbed coffee at home, and cruised to Victrola on my board through Capitol Hill—the sun was already hitting different. Work was chill until mid-morning when we all gathered around the TV for the USA match. The energy was electric, even if the game was kind of messy. I was hyped, talking trash with regulars, losing myself in it for a bit.

After my shift I grabbed some snacks at Whole Foods and headed back to sketch for a few hours. Just me, my markers, some hip-hop blasting—that's when I feel most myself, you know? Then met up with friends at Ground Kontrol downtown. The arcade was packed and loud and perfect. We played games until the sun started dropping, and the ride home through the warm evening felt like the cherry on top. Tired now, but the good kind.
thought_process: The World Cup match really pulled me in—I wasn't expecting to care that much, but having that communal moment at the shop with regulars felt grounding. It broke up the workday rhythm. After, I needed to decompress, so I skipped straight home instead of wandering. The art time was intentional; I've been neglecting my sketches lately and it felt necessary, like something I owed myself. By afternoon my energy shifted—I was ready to be around people again, so hitting Ground Kontrol with friends felt like the natural move. The transit closure didn't really affect me since I skateboard most places anyway. Overall, the sunny weather and the mix of solitude and social time kept me balanced. I ended the day feeling genuinely satisfied rather than just tired.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
